items.title: Latest LeBron James free agency news, rumors, updates on 'Decision'
items.summary: It's been 23 days and counting. And the NBA is still for LeBron James to make his decision.His agent, Klutch Sports CEO Rich Paul, has put out messages to teams suggesting they not wait for James and that they should fill their remaining roster spots.Those messages came after NBA Commissioner Adam Silver expressed his desire for James to decide so schedule makers can determine the 2026-27 regular season, including important dates such as opening night and Christmas Day.The idea is that James would be playing on those days, hence Silver's desire to know which team he'll choose. But James won't be rushed, according to Paul.It likely comes down to the Cleveland Cavaliers, Miami Heat and Philadelphia 76ers as the top three candidates to land James. However, the Golden State Warriors are not to be counted out yet.The Minnesota Timberwolves and Denver Nuggets also have been in the…

items.title: Swanson: LeBron James is taking his time team-hunting, but why are the Warriors an option?
items.summary: LeBron James, left, and Stephen Curry after a game at Chase Center in April. James is currently considering joining Curry and the Warriors next season. (Ezra Shaw / Getty Images)Take all the time you want, LeBron. It’s your life.Yes, it’s annoying to have to hear about it every day, but it’s your decision when to tell us where you’re going to play your 24th season of pro ball.So, go ahead, keep NBA schedule-makers on pins and needles. Savor having workers at my local Trader Joe’s discussing your future on a Monday in the middle of July. Enjoy your twilight limelight.But don’t tell us, on podcasts and in public appearances, that the factors you’re prioritizing are A) whether a team is built to win now; B) the proximity to your home in L.A.; and C) not money, but overall happiness — and then signal it might be the Golden State Warriors.The Cleveland Cavaliers? They check all the…

items.title: Mark Pope provides a practice report recapping the Las Vegas trip
items.summary: Dec 28, 2025; Paradise, Nevada, USA; The Welcome to Fabulous Las Vegas sign on the Las Vegas strip. Mandatory Credit: Kirby Lee-Imagn Images | Kirby Lee-Imagn Images Mark Pope recently did an interview with UK Sports Network and was asked numerous questions about the team’s trip to Las Vegas earlier this month. Pope provided some clarity on why the team went, what they did, and what came from the trip.When asked what went into the decision, Pope responded, “Well, during that week, Vegas is kind of the basketball capital of the world, and it’s a perfect fusion of Kentucky Basketball, and all the guys that we’re recruiting, and all the guys that have played here that are now playing in the NBA. “So, you bring the high school and the college and the NBA ranks together, it was perfect for us.”With the blend of that, Kentucky men’s basketball social media pages shared pictures showing the…

items.title: Lakers won't trade Bronny, 'more than happy' to keep James | Report
items.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers might no longer have LeBron James Sr. on their roster, but they still have junior and seem to want to keep him.There have been questions on what will happen with Bronny James since his dad decided to ditch Los Angeles and the Lakers for free agency. Well, the Lakers, to the surprise of some, have gone all in on Bronny and keeping him in LA.His contract with the Lakers became fully guaranteed for $2.3 million on June 30. The 21-year-old has a contract that will pay him that figure this year, while a team option to keep him for 2027-28 remains on his deal. Bronny wouldn't be a free agent until 2028. There is belief that the Lakers value Bronny and would not move on from him unless a real offer came to surface.During a radio appearance on the Brock and Alex Show on 97.1 The Fan LA, California Post reporter Khobi Price, who covers the Lakers, said during a…

items.title: Did a YouTube account spoil LeBron James’ free agency decision?
items.summary: LeBron James of the Los Angeles Lakers runs down court during the first half at Barclays Center, Tuesday, Feb. 3, 2026, in Brooklyn, NY. Is LeBron James taking his talents back to South Beach? What appears to be a mistake by one team might have spoiled the fun — or maybe not. The Heat’s YouTube page published and then quickly deleted a video titled “LeBron James Introductory press conference” along with the date of July 27, 2026. However, the Miami Herald reported that the team’s social media department accidentally published the video, which was being prepared in case he did sign with the Heat, not that the team had notice of James’ actual decision.James, as of the early hours on Wednesday morning, has not yet officially made his highly anticipated choice in free agency. The 41-year-old’s next move has been speculated on for weeks since it became clear he was not returning to the…

items.title: Heat confirm the LeBron James introductory press conference YouTube video was published accidentally after latest rumors
items.summary: No, a YouTube video didn't tip off ahead of time that LeBron James was signing with the Miami Heat. The Heat posted a pending video to YouTube titled "LeBron James Introductory Press Conference" with a date later this month on Tuesday evening. Naturally, as James has yet to announce where he is playing next season, social media ran wild with theories. It turns out, that video was simply a mistake. A Heat spokesperson told the Miami Herald's Anthony Chiang on Tuesday night that the video was "mistakenly made public" by the team's social media department for "the possibility of James eventually deciding to join the Heat this offseason." There is no validity to the video. Whoops.Fans discover the Miami Heat posted a scheduled live stream titled “LeBron James Introductory Press Conference” slated for July 27th on YouTube.It was quickly…

items.title: LeBron James to Miami Heat? YouTube page leaks future press conference
items.summary: LeBron James is going back to the Miami Heat ― at least that's what many are speculating after the organization's latest slip up. The Miami Heat's official YouTube page briefly leaked a live video stream titled "LeBron James Introductory Press Conference" that was set for July 27, 2026.It has since been unlisted and made private. It seems that the video has been momentarily taken down, but not fast enough for internet scourers to collect their screenshots, screen-records and begin running with the narrative that James is headed back to the 305. .oembed-frame {width:100%;height:100%;margin:0;border:0;}The Miami Heat YouTube channel just accidentally LEAKED the LeBron signing 😱😳🚨 pic.twitter.com/qqKjC5b70m— Basketball Forever (@bballforever_) July 22, 2026If it's true, then at least we know that there will be an announcement within the next six days. However, it seems that it was a…

items.title: Naismith Basketball Hall of Fame announces new Stephen Curry exhibit
items.summary: It's a no-brainer that whenever Golden State Warriors guard Stephen Curry decides to hang it up for good, he'll be inducted into the Naismith Basketball Hall of Fame on the first ballot.In case his career statistics and accolades didn't signal that, the Basketball Hall of Fame practically ensured it when they announced that the four-time NBA champion will have his own exhibit, marking the first time an active player was honored with a dedicated experience.The Naismith Basketball Hall of Fame will introduce its newest exhibit on Friday, July 24, which will be dedicated to the 12-time NBA All-Star, two-time MVP, Olympic gold medalist, four-time NBA champion and the undisputed greatest shooter of all time.This development is a part of the Naismith Basketball Hall of Fame acclaimed gallery called "The Vault."The exhibit is called "Stephen Curry: Beyond the…
